documentation.text:
ChargingEscrow — payment escrow for a single EV charging session.
 Lifecycle:
   CREATED    → deposit()         → FUNDED
   FUNDED     → verifyStation()   → AUTHORIZED  (hash matches)
   FUNDED     → refund()          → REFUNDED     (hash mismatch — called by backend)
   AUTHORIZED → startCharging()   → CHARGING
   CHARGING   → updateSoc()       (no state change — progress tracking only)
   CHARGING   → completeSession() → COMPLETED
   COMPLETED  → releaseFunds()    → RELEASED
   any live   → cancel()          → REFUNDED     (timeout / attack detected by backend)
 IMPORTANT: Hash verification and SoC logic live off-chain in your Spring backend.
 This contract only enforces the money rules and records state transitions.
 The backend calls verifyStation(), updateSoc(), completeSession(), etc.
 after performing its own checks — the contract trusts the operator (msg.sender checks).

EV driver deposits payment into escrow.
 Must be called with msg.value > 0 while state is CREATED.
 Transitions: CREATED → FUNDED

Backend calls this after verifying the live hash off-chain against
 FirmwareRegistry. If hashes match, session moves to AUTHORIZED.
 If they don't match, backend should call refund() instead.
 The liveHash parameter is stored for on-chain auditability — the actual
 comparison logic lives in your BlockchainServiceImpl / DigitalTwinServiceImpl.
 @param liveHash  The hash the station reported (0x-prefixed, sent as bytes32)
 Transitions: FUNDED → AUTHORIZED

Backend calls this when the OCPP transaction START event is received
 and the session is AUTHORIZED.
 @param _sessionId  OCPP transaction ID / session UUID
 Transitions: AUTHORIZED → CHARGING

Backend calls this when targetSoc is reached OR when the OCPP
 TransactionEvent END is received.
 Transitions: CHARGING → COMPLETED

Releases escrowed funds to the charger after session completes successfully.
 Transitions: COMPLETED → RELEASED

typeDescriptions.typeString: literal_string "ChargingEscrow: cannot refund in current state"

arguments.value: ChargingEscrow: cannot refund in current state

argumentTypes.typeString: literal_string "ChargingEscrow: cannot refund in current state"

documentation.text:
Refunds the buyer. Called by the backend when:
  - hash verification fails (TAMPERED / UNKNOWN_STATION)
  - anomaly detected by DigitalTwinServiceImpl (ALERT state)
 Can be called from FUNDED, AUTHORIZED, or CHARGING states.
 @param reason  Short human-readable reason string (stored in event log)

Emergency cancel — callable by operator if session times out or
 a critical anomaly is detected after CHARGING begins.
 Also callable by buyer after sessionTimeout elapses (trustless fallback).

params.reason: Short human-readable reason string (stored in event log)

params._sessionId: OCPP transaction ID / session UUID Transitions: AUTHORIZED → CHARGING

params.liveHash: The hash the station reported (0x-prefixed, sent as bytes32) Transitions: FUNDED → AUTHORIZED

cancel(string).notice: Emergency cancel — callable by operator if session times out or a critical anomaly is detected after CHARGING begins. Also callable by buyer after sessionTimeout elapses (trustless fallback).

completeSession().notice: Backend calls this when targetSoc is reached OR when the OCPP TransactionEvent END is received. Transitions: CHARGING → COMPLETED

deposit().notice: EV driver deposits payment into escrow. Must be called with msg.value > 0 while state is CREATED. Transitions: CREATED → FUNDED

refund(string).notice: Refunds the buyer. Called by the backend when:  - hash verification fails (TAMPERED / UNKNOWN_STATION)  - anomaly detected by DigitalTwinServiceImpl (ALERT state) Can be called from FUNDED, AUTHORIZED, or CHARGING states.

releaseFunds().notice: Releases escrowed funds to the charger after session completes successfully. Transitions: COMPLETED → RELEASED

startCharging(string).notice: Backend calls this when the OCPP transaction START event is received and the session is AUTHORIZED.

updateSoc(uint8).notice: Backend calls this periodically as MeterValues arrive over OCPP. No state transition — just updates currentSoc for on-chain auditability.

verifyStation(bytes32).notice: Backend calls this after verifying the live hash off-chain against FirmwareRegistry. If hashes match, session moves to AUTHORIZED. If they don't match, backend should call refund() instead. The liveHash parameter is stored for on-chain auditability — the actual comparison logic lives in your BlockchainServiceImpl / DigitalTwinServiceImpl.

userdoc.notice: ChargingEscrow — payment escrow for a single EV charging session. Lifecycle:   CREATED    → deposit()         → FUNDED   FUNDED     → verifyStation()   → AUTHORIZED  (hash matches)   FUNDED     → refund()          → REFUNDED     (hash mismatch — called by backend)   AUTHORIZED → startCharging()   → CHARGING   CHARGING   → updateSoc()       (no state change — progress tracking only)   CHARGING   → completeSession() → COMPLETED   COMPLETED  → releaseFunds()    → RELEASED   any live   → cancel()          → REFUNDED     (timeout / attack detected by backend) IMPORTANT: Hash verification and SoC logic live off-chain in your Spring backend. This contract only enforces the money rules and records state transitions. The backend calls verifyStation(), updateSoc(), completeSession(), etc. after performing its own checks — the contract trusts the operator (msg.sender checks).
